meta.title: Einier Freyre – Cloud & Platform Engineering
meta.description: Cloud, DevOps and Platform Engineering consulting for SMBs in LATAM. Infrastructure audits, CI/CD automation, Kubernetes, and Cloud cost reduction. Free initial diagnostic.
meta.ogDescription: Cloud, DevOps and Platform Engineering consulting for SMBs in LATAM. Automation, Kubernetes, cost reduction. Free initial diagnostic.

hero.title: Cloud & Platform Engineering
hero.subtitle: Your team deploys in minutes, your Cloud bill drops up to 35%, and your infrastructure stops being a headache.

about.text: I have 6+ years building backend software for startups and SMBs across LATAM — from technical leadership to Cloud and DevOps. I work as a fractional Platform Engineer: more affordable than a large consultancy, more experienced than a junior hire. No bureaucracy, no unnecessary jargon, with visible deliveries from week one.

grid.desc: Affordable model for SMBs · No minimum hours

problems.desc: If any of this sounds familiar, I can help:

cards.title: Slow deployments
cards.desc: Deploying takes hours or days. The process is manual, error-prone, and nobody wants to do it.

cards.title: Fragile Backend
cards.desc: Undocumented APIs, entangled microservices, untraceable errors. The backend slows everything down.

cards.title: Uncontrolled Cloud costs
cards.desc: The bill grows every month and nobody knows exactly why. Underutilized resources with no visibility.

cards.title: Stuck in technical debt
cards.desc: No tests, no patterns, every change feels risky. The team avoids refactoring and debt keeps piling up.

cards.title: Fear of deploying
cards.desc: Deploying to production is scary. No reliable rollbacks, no tests, no confidence.

cards.title: Team stuck in operations
cards.desc: The engineering team spends more time operating than building. Progress feels slow.

cards.title: Cloud Audit
cards.desc: Complete infrastructure review, risk and cost leak identification, and actionable recommendations.

cards.title: Backend Architecture
cards.desc: APIs, microservices and system design. From code review to complete architecture with Go, solid patterns and best practices.

cards.title: DevOps Implementation
cards.desc: CI/CD, automation, observability, containers and Kubernetes. Your team deploys with confidence in minutes.

cards.title: Fractional Platform Engineering
cards.desc: Ongoing support, platform improvements, strategic guidance and infrastructure evolution.

steps.title: Initial conversation
steps.desc: Free and no commitment. I learn about your situation, pain points, and what you need to achieve.

steps.title: Diagnostic
steps.desc: Review of your infrastructure, processes and current workflows. I identify concrete improvement opportunities.

steps.title: Proposal
steps.desc: I present a clear plan with scope, timeline and investment. No surprises.

steps.title: Implementation
steps.desc: I build, automate and document. I work iteratively with visible deliveries from the first week.

steps.title: Follow-up
steps.desc: Team training and ongoing support. The platform stays operational and your team becomes autonomous.

cards.text: We went from creating and configuring our servers manually to doing it automatically in 15 minutes. Infrastructure is now treated as code, and our team can focus on building, not operating.
cards.author: — David Ocampo

cards.text: The Cloud audit revealed cost leaks nobody had identified. We optimized resources, reduced the bill by 35% and gained full visibility into our infrastructure.
cards.author: — Humberto

cards.text: The software Einier built for our academy eliminated errors in payment and membership tracking. We now send automated notifications and the administrative team recovered hours previously lost in spreadsheets.
cards.author: — Athletica Academia

blog.desc: Notes on Platform Engineering, Cloud Infrastructure and DevOps in LATAM.

cards.title: Building a Kubernetes Operator in Go with Kubebuilder
cards.desc: Walkthrough on CRDs, finalizers and leader election.

cards.title: From 5 days to 1 hour: migrating to Infrastructure as Code
cards.desc: Terraform + Cloud Init and lessons learned along the way.

cards.title: Observability on a budget: Prometheus + Grafana
cards.desc: Production monitoring for small teams without overspending.

cards.title: Using Goroutines Correctly with pprof: Memory Leaks & Data Races
cards.desc: Guide to proper Goroutine usage in Go, detecting memory leaks with pprof and eliminating data races.

contact.text: Tell me what you need and I'll prepare a tailored proposal. The initial conversation is free and with no commitment.
